ratings.comment: Dr. Forest is a terrific lecturer. However, she also doesn't tend to use slides. This means studying off of the book that has a lot of extra information or off of your probably very messy notes taken from her whiteboard. Without the help of my friends on the problem sets, I would have struggled in this class. Confusing problems were also an issue.

Don't take this class in the Spring. Both professors are awful and do not give fair exams in the slightest. Exam averages were around a 60% and the material on the exam expects way too much from the students. Dr. Forest thinks we are all experts by the time the exam arrives, which is far from the case. There is no indication of what to study.

Lectures were long and sometimes boring but she tried to keep us engaged with in class activities. Its probably helpful to do the readings before class. Tests are pretty hard but similar to the assignments she gives.

Dr. Forest is very passionate about teaching. Open book tests, but the exams are really hard because the questions she writes really force you to understand the material inside and out and problem solve on the fly.  Good class, but be wary of it if you don't have a great background in prokaryotic systems because it can be overwhelming.

Many complained about her unrealistic expectations but I felt that this was nonsense. Dr. Forest was enthusiastic and helpful. Her lectures were also logically organized with a set of learning goals which helped immensely with organization of information. Exam was tough but nothing impossible given a fair amount of reviewing and making connections.

Dr. Forest is the most throroughly excited professor I've yet had. Scarcely a day goes by before she thinks of some new fun/relevant information to throw to the crowd. I'll admit, 'in-class' activities are not to my taste and her slides are sometimes hard to see, but DEFINITELY PRE-READ before class. She's very willing to help if you seek her out.
